answer: Use the J-hook or frame hook on the designated tow points. Always secure straps properly and avoid the bumper or suspension components.
Attach the wheel lift under the vehicle's approved lifting points. Make sure wheels are centered and locked before towing.
Use the correct straps or hooks for the vehicle type. Always check tension and ensure the vehicle is stable before transport.
Deploy the flatbed or rollback according to manufacturer instructions. Use winches and ramps safely, ensuring the vehicle is aligned and secure.
For tire changes, ensure the vehicle is on stable ground, parking brake engaged, and lug nuts properly torqued when reinstalling.
Use appropriate recovery techniques such as winching, soft straps, or dolly assistance. Never exceed equipment limits.
These situations are high-risk. Follow safety protocols, use proper stabilization equipment, and consider calling for specialized recovery assistance.
Check battery voltage and connections. Use manufacturer-approved procedures for jump-starting or battery replacement. Observe all safety protocols, especially with high-voltage systems.
Follow proper lockout procedures. Avoid damage to the vehicle and prioritize safety, especially if a child or pet is inside.
Always engage manufacturer-recommended transport mode before towing. Avoid contact with high-voltage components and follow EV-specific towing procedures.
Exercise extreme caution. Set up warning devices, wear PPE, and ensure the vehicle is stable before any recovery operation.
